id.ذِكْرُ اللَّهِ: Dzikrullah — di sini ditafsirkan sebagai Ahlulbait

ru.حُجَّةُ اللَّهِ: Довод Аллаха — Коран как аргумент Бога перед творением
ru.ذِكْرُ اللَّهِ: Поминание Аллаха — здесь истолковано как Ахл аль-Бейт

tr.حُجَّةُ اللَّهِ: Allah'ın hücceti — Kur'an'ın yaratılış üzerindeki delil olma vasfı
tr.ذِكْرُ اللَّهِ: Allah'ın zikri — Ehl-i Beyt anlamında yorumlanmıştır

ur.حُجَّةُ اللَّهِ: اللہ کی حجت — قرآن مجید کا مقام
ur.ذِكْرُ اللَّهِ: ذکرِ اللہ — یہاں اہلِ بیت کی طرف اشارہ

zh.حُجَّةُ اللَّهِ: 安拉的证据——《古兰经》作为真主对造物之论证的地位
zh.ذِكْرُ اللَّهِ: 念记安拉——此处被诠释为先知家眷

seo_questions.bn: ইমাম বাকির কি কিয়ামতের দিনে কোরআন সম্পর্কে বলেছেন?
seo_questions.de: Was sagte Imam al-Baqir über den Quran am Tag des Gerichts?
seo_questions.en: What does Imam al-Baqir say about the Quran on the Day of Judgment in Al-Kafi?
seo_questions.es: ¿Qué dijo el Imam al-Baqir sobre el Corán en el Día del Juicio?
seo_questions.fa: امام باقر (ع) درباره قرآن در روز قیامت چه فرمودند؟
seo_questions.fr: Qu'a dit l'Imam al-Baqir sur le Coran au Jour du Jugement ?
seo_questions.id: Apa yang dikatakan Imam al-Baqir tentang al-Quran pada Hari Kiamat?
seo_questions.ru: Что сказал Имам аль-Бакир о Коране в День Суда?
seo_questions.tr: İmam Bâkır Kıyamet'te Kur'an hakkında ne söylemiştir?
seo_questions.ur: امام باقر علیہ السلام نے قیامت کے دن قرآن کے بارے میں کیا فرمایا؟
seo_questions.zh: 巴基尔伊玛目关于复生日的《古兰经》说了什么？

similar_content_hints.description: Hadiths in al-Kafi and other sources describing the Quran as a living intercessor on the Day of Judgment who will advocate for its reciters before Allah.
Traditions from the Imams about the ontological status and living nature of the salat, describing it as an entity that enjoins good and forbids evil.
Narrations in which Imam al-Baqir or Imam al-Sadiq interpret Quranic verses about 'dhikr Allah' or 'ulu al-amr' as referring to the Ahl al-Bayt.

summaries.bn: ইমাম মুহাম্মাদ আল-বাকির (আ.) সা'দ আল-খাফফাফকে কিয়ামতের দিনে কোরআনের একটি বিস্তারিত দৃশ্য বর্ণনা করেছেন — কোরআন একটি জীবন্ত সত্তা হিসেবে বিভিন্ন রূপে মহশরের কাতারগুলো অতিক্রম করে এবং তার বিশ্বস্ত ধারকদের জন্য সুপারিশ করে।
summaries.de: Imam al-Baqir (Friede sei mit ihm) schilderte Sa'd al-Khaffaf eine ausgedehnte eschatologische Vision des Qurans als lebendige Wesenheit, die am Jüngsten Tag in verschiedenen Gestalten erscheint, die Reihen durchquert und für seine Treuen Fürsprache einlegt.
summaries.en: Imam al-Baqir (AS) narrated this extended eschatological vision to Sa'd al-Khaffaf, depicting the Quran as a living entity that will appear on the Day of Resurrection in the form of a man, progressively ascending through the rows of Muslims, martyrs, Prophets, and Angels — each group recognising it yet marvelling at its superior rank. The Quran intercedes for those who devoted their lives to it and the Imam reveals the living, speaking nature of both the Quran and the salat, concluding with a Quranic verse (29:45) reinterpreted to assert that the Ahl al-Bayt are 'the remembrance of Allah'.
summaries.es: El Imam al-Baqir (paz sea con él) narró a Sa'd al-Khaffaf una visión escatológica extensa del Corán como entidad viviente que se manifestará el Día del Juicio en múltiples formas, avanzando entre los grupos reunidos, intercediendo por sus devotos y finalmente recibiendo el honor divino.
summaries.fa: امام محمد باقر (ع) در این روایت طولانی به سعد الخفاف تصویر اسکاتولوژیکی از قرآن ارائه می‌دهد که در روز قیامت به صورت موجودی زنده در میان صفوف مختلف ظاهر می‌شود و برای حاملان خود شفاعت می‌کند.
summaries.fr: L'Imam al-Baqir (que la paix soit sur lui) a narré à Sa'd al-Khaffaf une longue vision eschatologique du Coran se manifestant comme une entité vivante le Jour du Jugement, parcourant les rangées de l'assemblée et intercédant pour ceux qui lui ont été fidèles.
summaries.id: Imam al-Baqir (as) meriwayatkan kepada Sa'd al-Khaffaf gambaran eskatologis yang rinci tentang al-Quran sebagai entitas hidup yang akan hadir pada Hari Kiamat, melewati berbagai kelompok di Padang Mahsyar, dan memberikan syafaat bagi para penghafalnya yang setia.
summaries.ru: Имам аль-Бакир (мир ему) рассказал Са'ду аль-Хаффафу обширное эсхатологическое видение, в котором Коран предстаёт как живое существо, проходящее в различных образах через ряды собравшихся в Судный день и заступающееся за своих верных.
summaries.tr: İmam Bâkır (a.s.), Sa'd el-Haffaf'a Kıyamet'te Kur'an'ın çeşitli suretlerde tezahür ederek safları geçeceğini ve sonunda Allah'ın huzurunda en yüce makamı elde edeceğini anlatan uzun bir hadis rivayet etmiştir. Hadis, Kur'an'ın hem ontolojik statüsünü hem de ona bağlı kalmış müminin ebedi ödülünü tasvir etmektedir.
summaries.ur: امام محمد باقر علیہ السلام نے سعد الخفاف کو قیامت کے دن قرآن کا ایک تفصیلی نقشہ بیان فرمایا جس میں قرآن ایک جاندار ہستی کے طور پر مختلف صورتوں میں ظاہر ہو کر ہر گروہ کو حیران کرتا ہے اور اپنے وفادار تلاوت کنندگان کی شفاعت کرتا ہے۔
summaries.zh: 巴基尔伊玛目（愿主赐他平安）向萨阿德·哈法法讲述了一段关于《古兰经》在复生日作为活体实体的宏大末世异象，描述其穿越各群体行列、为忠实诵读者代求的情景。

translation.en: then He calls out to him

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول أو ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/474)
text: يَا سَعْدُ تَعَلَّمُوا الْقُرْآنَ فَإِنَّ الْقُرْآنَ يَأْتِي يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ فِي أَحْسَنِ صُورَةٍ نَظَرَ إِلَيْهَا الْخَلْقُ وَ النَّاسُ صُفُوفٌ عِشْرُونَ وَ مِائَةُ أَلْفِ صَفٍّ ثَمَانُونَ أَلْفَ صَفٍّ أُمَّةُ مُحَمَّدٍ وَ أَرْبَعُونَ أَلْفَ صَفٍّ مِنْ سَائِرِ الْأُمَمِ

فَيَأْتِي عَلَى صَفِّ الْمُسْلِمِينَ فِي صُورَةِ رَجُلٍ فَيُسَلِّمُ فَيَنْظُرُونَ إِلَيْهِ ثُمَّ يَقُولُونَ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ الْحَلِيمُ الْكَرِيمُ إِنَّ هَذَا الرَّجُلَ مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ نَعْرِفُهُ بِنَعْتِهِ وَ صِفَتِهِ غَيْرَ أَنَّهُ كَانَ أَشَدَّ اجْتِهَاداً مِنَّا فِي الْقُرْآنِ فَمِنْ هُنَاكَ أُعْطِيَ مِنَ الْبَهَاءِ وَ الْجَمَالِ وَ النُّورِ مَا لَمْ نُعْطَهُ

ثُمَّ يُجَاوِزُ حَتَّى يَأْتِيَ عَلَى صَفِّ الشُّهَدَاءِ فَيَنْظُرُونَ إِلَيْهِ الشُّهَدَاءُ ثُمَّ يَقُولُونَ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ الرَّبُّ الرَّحِيمُ إِنَّ هَذَا الرَّجُلَ مِنَ الشُّهَدَاءِ نَعْرِفُهُ بِسَمْتِهِ وَ صِفَتِهِ غَيْرَ أَنَّهُ مِنْ شُهَدَاءِ الْبَحْرِ فَمِنْ هُنَاكَ أُعْطِيَ مِنَ الْبَهَاءِ وَ الْفَضْلِ مَا لَمْ نُعْطَهُ

قَالَ فَيَتَجَاوَزُ حَتَّى يَأْتِيَ عَلَى صَفِّ شُهَدَاءِ الْبَحْرِ فِي صُورَةِ شَهِيدٍ فَيَنْظُرُ إِلَيْهِ شُهَدَاءُ الْبَحْرِ فَيَكْثُرُ تَعَجُّبُهُمْ وَ يَقُولُونَ إِنَّ هَذَا مِنْ شُهَدَاءِ الْبَحْرِ نَعْرِفُهُ بِسَمْتِهِ وَ صِفَتِهِ غَيْرَ أَنَّ الْجَزِيرَةَ الَّتِي أُصِيبَ فِيهَا كَانَتْ أَعْظَمَ هَوْلًا مِنَ الْجَزِيرَةِ الَّتِي أُصِبْنَا فِيهَا فَمِنْ هُنَاكَ أُعْطِيَ مِنَ الْبَهَاءِ وَ الْجَمَالِ وَ النُّورِ مَا لَمْ نُعْطَهُ

ثُمَّ يُجَاوِزُ حَتَّى يَأْتِيَ صَفَّ النَّبِيِّينَ وَ الْمُرْسَلِينَ فِي صُورَةِ نَبِيٍّ مُرْسَلٍ فَيَنْظُرُ النَّبِيُّونَ وَ الْمُرْسَلُونَ إِلَيْهِ فَيَشْتَدُّ لِذَلِكَ تَعَجُّبُهُمْ وَ يَقُولُونَ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ الْحَلِيمُ الْكَرِيمُ إِنَّ هَذَا النَّبِيَّ مُرْسَلٌ نَعْرِفُهُ بِسَمْتِهِ وَ صِفَتِهِ غَيْرَ أَنَّهُ أُعْطِيَ فَضْلًا كَثِيراً

قَالَ فَيَجْتَمِعُونَ فَيَأْتُونَ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) فَيَسْأَلُونَهُ وَ يَقُولُونَ يَا مُحَمَّدُ مَنْ هَذَا فَيَقُولُ لَهُمْ أَ وَ مَا تَعْرِفُونَهُ فَيَقُولُونَ مَا نَعْرِفُهُ هَذَا مِمَّنْ لَمْ يَغْضَبِ اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ فَيَقُولُ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) هَذَا حُجَّةُ اللَّهِ عَلَى خَلْقِهِ فَيُسَلِّمُ

ثُمَّ يُجَاوِزُ حَتَّى يَأْتِيَ عَلَى صَفِّ الْمَلَائِكَةِ فِي سُورَةِ مَلَكٍ مُقَرَّبٍ فَتَنْظُرُ إِلَيْهِ الْمَلَائِكَةُ فَيَشْتَدُّ تَعَجُّبُهُمْ وَ يَكْبُرُ ذَلِكَ عَلَيْهِمْ لِمَا رَأَوْا مِنْ فَضْلِهِ وَ يَقُولُونَ تَعَالَى رَبُّنَا وَ تَقَدَّسَ إِنَّ هَذَا الْعَبْدَ مِنَ الْمَلَائِكَةِ نَعْرِفُهُ بِسَمْتِهِ وَ صِفَتِهِ غَيْرَ أَنَّهُ كَانَ أَقْرَبَ الْمَلَائِكَةِ إِلَى اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ مَقَاماً فَمِنْ هُنَاكَ أُلْبِسَ مِنَ النُّورِ وَ الْجَمَالِ مَا لَمْ نُلْبَسْ

ثُمَّ يُجَاوِزُ حَتَّى يَنْتَهِيَ إِلَى رَبِّ الْعِزَّةِ تَبَارَكَ وَ تَعَالَى فَيَخِرُّ تَحْتَ الْعَرْشِ فَيُنَادِيهِ تَبَارَكَ وَ تَعَالَى يَا حُجَّتِي فِي الْأَرْضِ وَ كَلَامِيَ الصَّادِقَ النَّاطِقَ ارْفَعْ رَأْسَكَ وَ سَلْ تُعْطَ وَ اشْفَعْ تُشَفَّعْ

فَيَرْفَعُ رَأْسَهُ فَيَقُولُ اللَّهُ تَبَارَكَ وَ تَعَالَى كَيْفَ رَأَيْتَ عِبَادِي فَيَقُولُ يَا رَبِّ مِنْهُمْ مَنْ صَانَنِي وَ حَافَظَ عَلَيَّ وَ لَمْ يُضَيِّعْ شَيْئاً وَ مِنْهُمْ مَنْ ضَيَّعَنِي وَ اسْتَخَفَّ بِحَقِّي وَ كَذَّبَ بِي وَ أَنَا حُجَّتُكَ عَلَى جَمِيعِ خَلْقِكَ
فَيَقُولُ اللَّهُ تَبَارَكَ وَ تَعَالَى وَ عِزَّتِي وَ جَلَالِي وَ ارْتِفَاعِ مَكَانِي لَأُثِيبَنَّ عَلَيْكَ الْيَوْمَ أَحْسَنَ الثَّوَابِ وَ لَأُعَاقِبَنَّ عَلَيْكَ الْيَوْمَ أَلِيمَ الْعِقَابِ

قَالَ فَيَرْجِعُ الْقُرْآنُ رَأْسَهُ فِي صُورَةٍ أُخْرَى قَالَ فَقُلْتُ لَهُ يَا أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ فِي أَيِّ صُورَةٍ يَرْجِعُ قَالَ فِي صُورَةِ رَجُلٍ شَاحِبٍ مُتَغَيِّرٍ يُبْصِرُهُ أَهْلُ الْجَمْعِ فَيَأْتِي الرَّجُلَ مِنْ شِيعَتِنَا الَّذِي كَانَ يَعْرِفُهُ وَ يُجَادِلُ بِهِ أَهْلَ الْخِلَافِ فَيَقُومُ بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ فَيَقُولُ مَا تَعْرِفُنِي فَيَنْظُرُ إِلَيْهِ الرَّجُلُ فَيَقُولُ مَا أَعْرِفُكَ يَا عَبْدَ اللَّهِ

قَالَ فَيَرْجِعُ فِي صُورَتِهِ الَّتِي كَانَتْ فِي الْخَلْقِ الْأَوَّلِ وَ يَقُولُ مَا تَعْرِفُنِي فَيَقُولُ نَعَمْ فَيَقُولُ الْقُرْآنُ أَنَا الَّذِي أَسْهَرْتُ لَيْلَكَ وَ أَنْصَبْتُ عَيْشَكَ سَمِعْتَ الْأَذَى وَ رُجِمْتَ بِالْقَوْلِ فِيَّ أَلَا وَ إِنَّ كُلَّ تَاجِرٍ قَدِ اسْتَوْفَى تِجَارَتَهُ وَ أَنَا وَرَاءَكَ الْيَوْمَ

قَالَ فَيَنْطَلِقُ بِهِ إِلَى رَبِّ الْعِزَّةِ تَبَارَكَ وَ تَعَالَى فَيَقُولُ يَا رَبِّ يَا رَبِّ عَبْدُكَ وَ أَنْتَ أَعْلَمُ بِهِ قَدْ كَانَ نَصِباً فِيَّ مُوَاظِباً عَلَيَّ يُعَادَى بِسَبَبِي وَ يُحِبُّ فِيَّ وَ يُبْغِضُ فَيَقُولُ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ أَدْخِلُوا عَبْدِي جَنَّتِي وَ اكْسُوهُ حُلَّةً مِنْ حُلَلِ الْجَنَّةَ وَ تَوِّجُوهُ بِتَاجٍ

فَإِذَا فُعِلَ بِهِ ذَلِكَ عُرِضَ عَلَى الْقُرْآنِ فَيُقَالُ لَهُ هَلْ رَضِيتَ بِمَا صُنِعَ بِوَلِيِّكَ فَيَقُولُ يَا رَبِّ إِنِّي أَسْتَقِلُّ هَذَا لَهُ فَزِدْهُ مَزِيدَ الْخَيْرِ كُلِّهِ فَيَقُولُ وَ عِزَّتِي وَ جَلَالِي وَ عُلُوِّي وَ ارْتِفَاعِ مَكَانِي لَأَنْحَلَنَّ لَهُ الْيَوْمَ خَمْسَةَ أَشْيَاءَ مَعَ الْمَزِيدِ لَهُ وَ لِمَنْ كَانَ بِمَنْزِلَتِهِ أَلَا إِنَّهُمْ شَبَابٌ لَا يَهْرَمُونَ وَ أَصِحَّاءُ لَا يَسْقُمُونَ وَ أَغْنِيَاءُ لَا يَفْتَقِرُونَ وَ فَرِحُونَ لَا يَحْزَنُونَ وَ أَحْيَاءٌ لَا يَمُوتُونَ

ثُمَّ تَلَا هَذِهِ الْآيَةَ لا يَذُوقُونَ فِيهَا الْمَوْتَ إِلَّا الْمَوْتَةَ الْأُولى قَالَ قُلْتُ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ يَا أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ وَ هَلْ يَتَكَلَّمُ الْقُرْآنُ فَتَبَسَّمَ ثُمَّ قَالَ رَحِمَ اللَّهُ الضُّعَفَاءَ مِنْ شِيعَتِنَا إِنَّهُمْ أَهْلُ تَسْلِيمٍ ثُمَّ قَالَ نَعَمْ يَا سَعْدُ وَ الصَّلَاةُ تَتَكَلَّمُ وَ لَهَا صُورَةٌ وَ خَلْقٌ تَأْمُرُ وَ تَنْهَى

قَالَ سَعْدٌ فَتَغَيَّرَ لِذَلِكَ لَوْنِي وَ قُلْتُ هَذَا شَيْ‏ءٌ لَا أَسْتَطِيعُ أَنَا أَتَكَلَّمُ بِهِ فِي النَّاسِ فَقَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ وَ هَلِ النَّاسُ إِلَّا شِيعَتُنَا فَمَنْ لَمْ يَعْرِفِ الصَّلَاةَ فَقَدْ أَنْكَرَ حَقَّنَا
ثُمَّ قَالَ يَا سَعْدُ أُسْمِعُكَ كَلَامَ الْقُرْآنِ قَالَ سَعْدٌ فَقُلْتُ بَلَى صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْكَ فَقَالَ .
إِنَّ الصَّلاةَ تَنْهى عَنِ الْفَحْشاءِ وَ الْمُنْكَرِ وَ لَذِكْرُ اللَّهِ أَكْبَرُ
فَالنَّهْيُ كَلَامٌ وَ الْفَحْشَاءُ وَ الْمُنْكَرُ رِجَالٌ وَ نَحْنُ ذِكْرُ اللَّهِ وَ نَحْنُ أَكْبَرُ

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al Abbas, from Al Husayn Bin Abdul Rahman, from Sufyan Al Hareyri, from his father, from Sa’ad Al Khaffaf,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> having said: ‘O Sa’ad! Learn the Quran, for the Quran would come on the Day of Judgment in an excellent image. The creatures would look at it, and the people would be in one hundred and twenty thousand rows – eighty thousand rows being the community of Muhammad<sup style="font-weight: normal">saww</sup>, and forty thousand rows from the rest of the communities.

So it would come to the rows of the Muslims in an image of a man, and it would greet. So they would be looking at him, then they would be saying, ‘There is no god except Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup>, the Forbearing, the Benevolent. This man is from the Muslims. We recognise him by his appearance and his description, apart from that he was of a more intense striving than us in the Quran, so from over there he has been Given from the splendour, and the beauty, and the radiance what we have not been Given’.

Then he would cross over until he comes to the rows of the martyrs. So the martyrs would be looking at him, then they would be saying, ‘There is no god except Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup>, the Lord<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup>, the Merciful. This man is from the martyrs. We recognise him by his appearance and his qualities, apart from that he is from the martyrs of the sea, for from over there he has been Given from the splendour and the superiority what he was not been Given’.

He<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> said: ‘So he would cross over until he comes to the rows of the martyrs of the sea in an image of a martyr. So the martyrs of the sea would look at him and would frequent in wonderment, and they would be saying, ‘This one is from the martyrs of the sea. We recognise him by his appearance and his qualities, apart from that the island in which he was hit would have been of a more grievous terror than the island in which we were hit. So from over there, he was Given from the splendour, and the beauty, and the radiance what we have not been Given’.

Then he would cross over until he comes to the rows of the Prophetsas and the Mursil Prophetsas in an image of a Mursil Prophetas. So the Prophetsas and the Mursil Prophetsas would be looking at him, and theiras wonderment would intensify due to that, and they would be saying: ‘There is no god except Allahazwj, the Forbearing, the Benevolent. This is a Mursil Prophet. We recognise him by his appearance and his qualities, apart from that he has been Given a lot of merits’.

So they<sup style="font-weight: normal">as</sup> would be gathering together, and they<sup style="font-weight: normal">as</sup> would be coming to Rasool-Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">saww</sup>, and they<sup style="font-weight: normal">as</sup> would be asking him<sup style="font-weight: normal">saww</sup>: ‘O Muhammad<sup style="font-weight: normal">saww</sup>! Who is this?’ So he<sup style="font-weight: normal">saww</sup> would be saying to them: ‘Or are you<sup style="font-weight: normal">as</sup> all not recognising him?’ So they<sup style="font-weight: normal">as</sup> would be saying: ‘We<sup style="font-weight: normal">as</sup> do not recognise him. This is from the one whom Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> is not Angered upon’. So Rasool-Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">saww</sup> would be saying: ‘This is a Proof of Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> upon His<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> creatures, so greet him’.

Then he would cross over until he comes to the rows of the Angels in an image of an Angel of Proximity. So the Angels would look at him and their wonderment would intensify, and that would be a great thing upon them due to what they see from his merits, and they would be saying: ‘Exalted is our Lord<sup>azwj</sup> and Holy! This is the servant from the Angels. We recognise him by his appearance and his qualities, apart from that he was the closest of the Angels to Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic in status, so from over there he was Clothed from the radiance and the beauty what we have not been Clothed in’.

Then he would cross over until he ends up to the Lord<sup>azwj</sup> of the Might, the Blessed and Exalted. So he would prostrate beneath the Throne. So the Blessed and High would Call out to him: “O My<sup>azwj</sup> Proof in the earth, and My<sup>azwj</sup> Truthful Speech, the Speaker! Raise your head and ask, you will be Given, and interceded, your intercession will be Accepted!”

So he would raise his head, and Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Blessed and High would be saying: “How did you see My<sup>azwj</sup> servants to be?” So he would be saying: ‘O Lord<sup>azwj</sup>! From them were the ones who safeguarded me and memorised me, and did not waste anything; and from them were the one who wasted me and took lightly with my rights, and belief with me, and I am Your<sup>azwj</sup> Proof upon the entirety of Your<sup>azwj</sup> creatures’.

So Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Blessed and High would be Saying: “By My<sup>azwj</sup> Might and the Loftiness of My<sup>azwj</sup> Position! Today I<sup>azwj</sup> shall be Rewarding with the most excellent of Rewards based upon you, and today I<sup>azwj</sup> shall be Punishing with the most painful of the Punishments based upon you”.

He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘So the Quran would be returning its head to be in another image’. I said to him<sup>asws</sup>, ‘O Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup>! In which image would it return to be?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘In a changed image of a pale man. The people gathered would look at him, and he would go over to a man from our Shias whom he would recognise the one who used to argue by him against the adversaries. So he would pause in front of him and he would be saying: ‘Do you not recognise me?’ So the man would look at him and he would be saying, ‘I do not recognise you, O servant of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>!’

He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘So it would return to be in its image which it used to be among the former creatures, and it would be saying: ‘Do you not recognise me (even now)?’ So he would be saying, ‘Yes (I do now)’. So the Quran would be saying: ‘I am that which you held a vigil with for your nights, and your life was concentrated upon listening to the hurtful things and you were pelted with the words regarding me. Indeed! And every trader would be fulfilled his trade, and I would be your backer today’.

He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘So it would go with him to the Lord<sup>azwj</sup> of Might, the Blessed and Exalted, and it would be saying: ‘O Lord<sup>azwj</sup>! O Lord<sup>azwj</sup>! Your<sup>azwj</sup> servant, and You<sup>azwj</sup> are more Knowing with him, he was hard-working regarding me, and had established animosity due to my reason, and he was loving and hating regarding me. So Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic would be Saying: “Enter My<sup>azwj</sup> servant into My<sup>azwj</sup> Paradise, and Clothe him with garments and ornaments of the Paradise, and crown him with a crown’.

So when that would be done with him, he would be displayed to the Quran, so He<sup>azwj</sup> would be Saying to it: ‘Are you pleased with what I<sup>azwj</sup> have done with your friend?’ So it would be saying: ‘O Lord<sup>azwj</sup>! I consider this to be little for him, therefore Increase it more with the goodness, all of it’. So He<sup>azwj</sup> would be Saying: “By My<sup>azwj</sup> Might and My<sup>azwj</sup> Majesty and My<sup>azwj</sup> Exaltedness and the Loftiness of My<sup>azwj</sup> Position! I<sup>azwj</sup> shall Permit five things to be for him, along with the increase for him and for the ones who were at his status. Indeed! He shall be a youth and would not be growing old, and he shall be healthy and would not be getting any sickness, and he shall be rich and would not be impoverished, and he shall be happy and would not be grieving, and he shall live and would not be dying”.

Then the Imamasws recited this Verse [44:56] They shall not taste therein death except for the first death. I said, ‘May I be sacrificed for youasws, O Abu Ja’farasws! And would the Quran be speaking?’ So heasws smiled, then said: ‘May Allahazwj be Merciful upon the weak ones (of understanding) from ourasws Shias. They are people of the submission’. Then heasws said: ‘Yes, O Sa’ad! And the Salāt would (also) be speaking, and for it there would be an image, and a shape. It would enjoin and forbid’.

Sa’ad said, ‘So my colour changed due to that, and I said, ‘This is something I do not have the capacity to speak with among the people’. So Abu Ja’farasws said: ‘And are the people except for ourasws Shias? So the one who does not recognise the Salāt, so he has denied ourasws rights’.
Then he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘O Sa’ad! Shall I<sup>asws</sup> make you hear the speech of the Quran?’ So I said, ‘Yes, O son<sup>asws</sup> of Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup>!’ So he<sup>asws</sup> said:
‘[29:45] surely, Salāt prevents from the immoralities and evil, and the Remembrance of Allah is the greatest.
So the prevention is a speech, and the immoralities and the evil are (two) men, and we<sup>asws</sup> are the Remembrance of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, and we<sup>asws</sup> are the greatest’.
en.sarwar: 1. Ali ibn Muhammad has narrated from al-‘Abbas from h n Fadl ibn Shadhan ‘Abd al-Rahman from Sufyan al-Hariri from his father from Sa’d al-Khaffaf from abu Ja’far (a.s.) who has said the following: “O Sa’d, learn the Holy Quran; on the Day of Judgment the Holy Quran will come in the best form that people may have ever seen. People will be present in one hundred thousand and twenty rows: the followers of Prophet Muhammad will form eighty thousand rows. The other forty thousand rows will consist of the followers of the other prophets. The Holy Quran will come to the rows of the Muslims in the form of a well-shaped man, offer them the greeting of peace and they look to him and say to each other, ‘No one deserves to be worshipped except Allah, the forbearing the magnanimous, this man is one of the Muslims whom we know from his picture and qualities, however, he has been striving about the Holy Quran much harder than we, thus, he has been given this beauty, grace and light which we have not received. “He (the Quran in the form of a person) passes toward the row of the martyrs who look at him and say, ‘No one deserves to be worshipped except Allah, the Lord, the Merciful. We know from the signs and qualities he is a martyr, however, he has been of the martyrs at sea, thus, he has received this beauty and grace that is not given to us.’ The Imam said, ‘Then it will pass toward the martyr at sea and the martyrs at sea look at him with astonishment and say, “He is of the martyrs at sea; we know him from his signs and qualities, however, on the island where he suffered must have been much more dangerous than the island where we suffered, thus, he has received this beauty, grace and light.’ “Then he approaches the rows of the prophets and messengers in the form of a messenger prophet. The prophets and messengers look at him and their astonishment increase. They will say, ‘No one deserves to be worshipped except Allah, the Most Forbearing, the Magnanimous, this is a messenger prophet; we know him from his picture and qualities, however, he has been much more virtuous.’ The Imam said, ‘They will come to the Messenger of Allah and ask him, “O Muhammad, who is this?” He then will ask, ‘Do you not know him?’ They will say, ‘We do not know him, however, Allah has not been angry with him.’ The Messenger of Allah will say to them, ‘This is the authority of Allah over His creatures. He offers them greeting of peace and passes thereby to the rows of the angels in the form of a special angel. The angels will look at him and their astonishment increases, his status will seem very great to them. They will say our Lord is Most High, Most Holy. This servant is of the angels. We know him from his picture and qualities, however, he has been more close and special to Allah, the Most Majestic, the Most Holy, thus, he is dressed up with this much light and beauty that we have not received.’ Then he will come before Allah, the Most Majestic, the Most Blessed, the Most High, and will bow down below the Throne. “Allah, the Most Blessed, the Most High, will speak to the Holy Quran saying, ‘My true spoken words and authority on earth, look up and ask for your wishes. Your wishes shall be granted and your intercession shall be accepted. How did you find my servants?’ “The Holy Quran will then say, ‘My Lord, certain ones among them kept me safely protected and did not lose anything from me but others among them lost me and disrespected me and called me lies even though I was Your authority to all of Your creatures.’ Allah, the Most Majestic, the Most Holy, then will say, ‘I swear by My Majesty, Greatness and Highness that I shall give the best reward on this day because of you and shall punish severely because of you.’ “The Imam then continued saying, ‘The Holy Quran will raise his head in another shape.’ I (the narrator) asked the Imam, ‘O abu Ja’far in what shape it will return?’ The Imam said, ‘It will come in the shape of a changed man, slim and tired looking. People in the gathering will see him. Then he will come forward to one of our followers who knew it and would argue with it against the people, opposed to ‘A’immah. The Holy Quran will ask him, ‘Do you know me’? The man will look it (the Holy Quran) and say, ‘O servant of Allah, I do not know you.’ The Holy Quran then will return in his former shape with which it existed among people, and will ask, ‘Do you still not know me’? He will say, ‘Yes, I do know you.’ It will say, ‘I am the Holy Quran for which you kept the whole night awake and faced suffering in your life, hearing disappointing words from people when you spoke from me. Obviously every merchant enjoys the benefit of his trade. Today I am behind you with full support. The Holy Quran will then proceed with him to the Throne of the Lord of Majesty, the Most Blessed, the Most High, and the Quran then will say, ‘Lord, this servant of You was very close to me and very careful about me. He would love others because of me and hate others just because of me.’ “Allah, the Most Majestic, the Most Holy, will then say, ‘Allow my servant enter paradise and allow him be dressed and crowned and then let him come back to see his friend, the Quran.’ When that servant of Allah will be well rewarded he will then come back to see his friend the Holy Quran, Allah, the Most High will ask the Holy Quran, ‘Are you happy with Our rewards given to your friend?’ the Holy Quran will say, ‘Lord, I consider it is not enough and I request that he should receive more of all the good things. Allah then will say, ‘I swear by My Majesty, Honor and Highness, I shall reward him and others like him with five things and more: I shall give them eternal youth, good health free from illness, wealth free from poverty, happiness free from sadness and life free from death.’ “The Imam then read this verse: ‘They will not experience any death other than that which they have already been through.’ ( 44 : 56 ) “I (the narrator) then said to abu Ja’far (a.s.) ‘May Allah keep my soul in service for your cause, does the Holy Quran speak?’ The Imam smiled and said, ‘May Allah grant favors upon our weak Shi’a (followers), they are people of submission.’ Then he said, ‘Yes, O Sa’D, prayer speaks. It has a form and shape. It commands and prohibits.’ Sa’D then said, ‘It changed my color and I said, “I will not be able to speak of this among people.” Abu Ja’far (a.s.) said, ‘Are people anything else besides our Shi’a (followers)? Those who do not know the right of the prayer, they have denied our rights.’ The Imam then said, ‘O Sa’d do you want, I will make you hear the words of the Holy Quran?’ I said, ‘Yes, may Allah grant you favors.’ The Imam said, ‘Prayer prohibits indecency and unlawful matters and speaking of Allah is greater,’ “prohibition” is speaking, (stopping people from going to) “indecency and unlawful matters” (that) are (nothing else but certain) men. We are the Dhikr of Allah, speaking of Allah, and we are greater.’” (The Holy Quran prohibits people from following certain people called indecency and unlawful matters)

translations.bn: আলী ইবনে ইব্রাহীম তাঁর পিতা থেকে, তিনি আন-নাওফালী থেকে, তিনি আস-সাকুনী থেকে, তিনি আবু আবদিল্লাহ থেকে, তিনি তাঁর পূর্বপুরুষগণ আলাইহিমুস সালাম থেকে বর্ণনা করেছেন যে তিনি বলেছেন:
translations.de: Ali ibn Ibrahim, von seinem Vater, von al-Nawfali, von al-Sakuni, von Abu Abdillah, von seinen Vorvätern (Friede sei mit ihnen), berichtete:
translations.en: Ali ibn Ibrahim, from his father, from al-Nawfali, from al-Sakuni, from Abu Abdillah, from his forefathers (peace be upon them), who said:
translations.es: Ali ibn Ibrahim, de su padre, de al-Nawfali, de al-Sakuni, de Abu Abdillah, de sus antepasados (la paz sea sobre ellos), dijo:
translations.fa: علی بن ابراهیم از پدرش، از النوفلی، از السکونی، از ابوعبدالله، از اجدادش علیهم السلام روایت کرد که فرمود:
translations.fr: Ali ibn Ibrahim, de son père, d'al-Nawfali, d'al-Sakuni, d'Abu Abdillah, de ses ancêtres (que la paix soit sur eux), rapporta :
translations.id: Ali bin Ibrahim dari ayahnya, dari al-Nawfali, dari al-Sakuni, dari Abu Abdillah, dari leluhurnya (alaihimus salam), berkata:
translations.ru: Али ибн Ибрахим передал от своего отца, тот от ан-Навфали, тот от ас-Сакуни, тот от Абу Абдиллаха, тот от его предков (мир им), что сказал:
translations.tr: Ali b. İbrahim, babasından, o en-Nevfeli'den, o es-Sekuni'den, o Ebu Abdullah'tan, o atalarından (a.s.) rivayet etti ki buyurdu:
translations.ur: علی بن ابراہیم نے اپنے والد سے، انہوں نے النوفلی سے، انہوں نے السکونی سے، انہوں نے ابو عبد اللہ سے، انہوں نے اپنے آباء و اجداد علیہم السلام سے روایت کی کہ انہوں نے فرمایا:
translations.zh: 阿里·本·易卜拉欣从他的父亲，从诺法利，从萨库尼，从阿布·阿卜杜拉，从其祖先（愿主赐他们平安）传述说：

translations.bn: আল্লাহর রাসূল সাল্লাল্লাহু আলাইহি ওয়া আলিহি বলেছেন: 'হে মানুষেরা! তোমরা একটি যুদ্ধবিরতির গৃহে আছ এবং তোমরা একটি সফরের পিঠে আছ, আর তোমাদের সাথে যাত্রা দ্রুত। তোমরা রাত ও দিন, সূর্য ও চাঁদকে দেখেছ, যারা প্রতিটি নতুন জিনিসকে পুরানো করে, প্রতিটি দূরবর্তী বিষয়কে নিকটে আনে, এবং প্রতিটি প্রতিশ্রুত বিষয় নিয়ে আসে। তাই দীর্ঘ পথের জন্য সফরসামগ্রী প্রস্তুত কর।'
translations.de: Der Gesandte Gottes (Friede sei mit ihm und seiner Familie) sagte: 'O Menschen! Ihr befindet euch in einem Haus des Waffenstillstands, und ihr seid auf dem Rücken einer Reise, und die Fahrt mit euch ist schnell. Ihr habt die Nacht und den Tag, die Sonne und den Mond gesehen, die jede neue Sache abnutzen, jede entfernte Sache näherbringen und jede versprochene Sache herbringen. So bereitet die Reiseausrüstung für die Länge des Übergangs vor.'
translations.en: The Messenger of God (peace be upon him and his family) said: 'O people! You are in a house of truce and you are upon the back of a journey, and the travel with you is swift. You have seen the night and the day, the sun and the moon, wearing out everything new and bringing near everything distant, and bringing every appointed thing. So prepare the provisions for the length of the passage.'
translations.es: El Mensajero de Dios (la paz sea sobre él y su familia) dijo: '¡Oh gente! Estáis en una casa de tregua y estáis sobre el dorso de un viaje, y el viaje con vosotros es veloz. Habéis visto la noche y el día, el sol y la luna, desgastando toda cosa nueva, acercando toda cosa lejana, y trayendo todo lo prometido. Así que preparad los avíos para la extensión del trayecto.'
translations.fa: رسول خدا صلی الله علیه وآله فرمود: «ای مردم! شما در خانه هدنه هستید و بر پشت سفر، و سیر با شما سریع است. شب و روز و خورشید و ماه را دیدید که هر نویی را کهنه می‌کنند، هر دوری را نزدیک می‌آورند و هر موعودی را می‌آورند. پس برای دوری گذرگاه توشه آماده کنید.»
translations.fr: Le Messager de Dieu (que la paix soit sur lui et sa famille) dit : 'Ô gens ! Vous êtes dans une demeure de trêve et vous êtes sur le dos d'un voyage, et le parcours avec vous est rapide. Vous avez vu la nuit et le jour, le soleil et la lune, user toute chose nouvelle, rapprocher toute chose lointaine, et apporter tout ce qui est promis. Préparez donc les provisions pour la longueur du passage.'
translations.id: Rasulullah (shallallahu alaihi wa alihi) bersabda: 'Wahai manusia! Kalian berada di negeri gencatan senjata (hudnah) dan kalian di atas punggung perjalanan; perjalanan itu berjalan cepat bersama kalian. Kalian telah melihat malam dan siang, matahari dan bulan, keduanya melapukkan setiap yang baru, mendekatkan setiap yang jauh, dan mendatangkan setiap yang dijanjikan. Maka siapkanlah bekal untuk panjangnya perjalanan.'
translations.ru: Посланник Аллаха (мир ему и его семье) сказал: «О люди! Вы находитесь в доме перемирия и на спине путешествия, и движение с вами стремительно. Вы видели ночь и день, солнце и луну, которые изнашивают каждую новую вещь, приближают каждую далёкую и приносят каждое обещанное. Так что готовьте снаряжение для длинного перехода.»
translations.tr: Allah'ın Rasulü (s.a.v.s.) buyurdu: 'Ey insanlar! Siz bir mütareke yurdundasınız ve bir seferin sırtındasınız; sizinle birlikte yolculuk süratlidir. Geceyi, gündüzü, güneşi ve ayı görürsünüz; her yeniyi eskitirler, her uzağı yakınlaştırırlar ve her vaadedileni getirirler. Öyleyse uzun yol için teçhizatı hazırlayın.'
translations.ur: رسول اللہ صلی اللہ علیہ وآلہ وسلم نے فرمایا: 'اے لوگو! تم ایک ہدنے کے گھر میں ہو اور تم سفر کی پشت پر ہو، اور تمہارا سفر تیز ہے۔ تم نے رات اور دن، سورج اور چاند کو دیکھا جو ہر نئی چیز کو پرانا کرتے ہیں، ہر دور چیز کو قریب لاتے ہیں، اور ہر وعدہ کی گئی چیز کو لاتے ہیں۔ پس لمبے راستے کے لیے زادِ سفر تیار کرو۔'
translations.zh: 安拉的使者（愿主赐福于他及其家人）说："啊，人们！你们处于一个停战的家园，你们在旅途的背上，旅行与你们同行迅速。你们已见过黑夜与白昼、太阳与月亮，它们使每件新事物老旧，使每件遥远之事接近，并带来每一件许诺之事。所以为漫长的旅途准备行装吧。"

translations.bn: বর্ণনাকারী বলেন: তারপর আল-মিকদাদ ইবনে আল-আসওয়াদ উঠে দাঁড়ালেন এবং বললেন: 'হে আল্লাহর রাসূল! যুদ্ধবিরতির গৃহ কী?' তিনি বললেন: 'বার্তা পৌঁছানো ও বিচ্ছিন্নতার গৃহ। যখন ফিতনাসমূহ অন্ধকার রাতের টুকরার মতো তোমাদের উপর এলোমেলো হয়ে পড়বে, তখন কুরআন আঁকড়ে ধরো; কারণ এটি এমন একজন সুপারিশকারী যার সুপারিশ গৃহীত হয়, এবং এমন একজন অভিযোগকারী যার অভিযোগ সত্যায়িত হয়। যে এটিকে নিজের সামনে রাখবে, এটি তাকে জান্নাতে নিয়ে যাবে; যে এটিকে পেছনে ছেড়ে দেবে, এটি তাকে জাহান্নামের দিকে হাঁকিয়ে নেবে। এটি সেই পথপ্রদর্শক যা সর্বোত্তম পথের দিক নির্দেশ করে।'
translations.de: Der Erzähler sagte: Dann stand al-Miqdad ibn al-Aswad auf und sagte: 'O Gesandter Gottes! Was ist das Haus des Waffenstillstands?' Er sagte: 'Ein Haus der Übermittlung und des Abbruchs. Wenn die Versuchungen euch wie Fetzen einer dunklen Nacht verwirren, so haltet euch an den Quran, denn er ist ein Fürsprecher, dessen Fürsprache angenommen wird, und ein Ankläger, dessen Anklage bestätigt wird. Wer ihn vor sich stellt, wird er ins Paradies führen; wer ihn hinter sich lässt, wird er in die Hölle treiben. Er ist der Leitfaden, der zum besten Weg führt.'
translations.en: He (the narrator) said: Then al-Miqdad ibn al-Aswad stood up and said: 'O Messenger of God, and what is the house of truce?' He said: 'A house of communication and severance. When the tribulations confound you like patches of dark night, then cling to the Quran, for it is an intercessor whose intercession is accepted, and an accuser who is ratified. Whoever places it before him, it guides him to Paradise; whoever places it behind him, it drives him to the Fire. It is the guide that points to the best path.'
translations.es: Dijo el narrador: Entonces se levantó al-Miqdad ibn al-Aswad y dijo: '¡Oh Mensajero de Dios! ¿Y qué es la casa de tregua?' Dijo: 'Una casa de comunicación y ruptura. Cuando las tribulaciones os confundan como fragmentos de noche oscura, entonces aferraos al Corán, pues es un intercesor cuya intercesión es aceptada, y un acusador que es ratificado. Quien lo ponga delante de él, lo guiará al Paraíso; quien lo ponga detrás, lo empujará hacia el Fuego. Es la guía que señala el mejor camino.'
translations.fa: راوی گفت: سپس مقداد بن اسود برخاست و گفت: «ای رسول خدا! خانه هدنه چیست؟» فرمود: «خانه‌ای برای ابلاغ و انقطاع. پس هنگامی که فتنه‌ها مانند پاره‌های شب تاریک بر شما مشتبه شدند، بر شما باد به قرآن، زیرا آن شفیعی است که شفاعتش پذیرفته می‌شود و شاکی‌ای است که تصدیق می‌گردد. هر که آن را پیش روی خود قرار دهد، او را به بهشت هدایت کند؛ هر که آن را پشت سر گذارد، او را به آتش براند. آن راهنمایی است که به بهترین راه راهنمایی می‌کند.»
translations.fr: Le narrateur dit : Puis al-Miqdad ibn al-Aswad se leva et dit : 'Ô Messager de Dieu ! Et qu'est-ce que la demeure de trêve ?' Il dit : 'Une demeure de communication et de rupture. Lorsque les épreuves vous troublent comme les lambeaux d'une nuit sombre, tenez-vous au Coran, car il est un intercesseur dont l'intercession est acceptée, et un accusateur dont l'accusation est ratifiée. Celui qui le place devant lui, il le mène au Paradis ; celui qui le place derrière lui, il le pousse vers le Feu. Il est le guide qui indique le meilleur chemin.'
translations.id: Ia berkata: Lalu al-Miqdad bin al-Aswad berdiri dan berkata: 'Wahai Rasulullah, apakah negeri gencatan senjata itu?' Beliau bersabda: 'Negeri penyampaian dan pemutusan. Jika fitnah-fitnah mengelabui kalian seperti potongan malam yang gelap, maka wajib atas kalian berpegang kepada al-Quran, karena sesungguhnya ia adalah pemberi syafaat yang syafaatnya diterima, dan penuntut yang diakui. Barangsiapa yang menjadikannya di depannya, ia akan menuntunnya ke surga; dan barangsiapa yang menjadikannya di belakangnya, ia akan mendorongnya ke neraka. Ia adalah petunjuk yang menunjuk kepada jalan terbaik.'
translations.ru: Рассказчик сказал: Затем аль-Микдад ибн аль-Асвад встал и сказал: «О Посланник Аллаха! Что такое дом перемирия?» Он сказал: «Дом передачи и разрыва. Когда смуты охватят вас как куски тёмной ночи, держитесь за Коран, ибо он — заступник, чьё заступничество принято, и обвинитель, чьё обвинение подтверждено. Тот, кто поставит его перед собой, он поведёт его в Рай; тот, кто оставит его позади, он погонит его к Огню. Он — проводник, указывающий наилучший путь.»
translations.tr: Ravi dedi ki: Sonra el-Mikdad b. el-Esved kalktı ve dedi: 'Ey Allah'ın Rasulü! Mütareke yurdu nedir?' Buyurdu: 'Tebliğ ve kopuşun yurdudur. Fitneler karanlık gecenin parçaları gibi üzerinize çöktüğünde, Kur'an'a sarılın; zira o, şefaati kabul edilen bir şefaatçidir ve doğrulanan bir iddiacıdır. Kim onu önüne koyarsa, onu cennete götürür; kim onu arkasına bırakırsa, onu ateşe sürükler. O, en iyi yolu gösteren bir rehberdir.'
translations.ur: راوی نے کہا: پھر مقداد بن اسود کھڑے ہوئے اور کہا: 'یا رسول اللہ! ہدنے کا گھر کیا ہے؟' آپ نے فرمایا: 'یہ پیغام پہنچانے اور انقطاع کا گھر ہے۔ جب فتنے تاریک رات کے ٹکڑوں کی طرح تم پر چھا جائیں، تو قرآن کو تھامو، کیونکہ وہ شافع ہے جس کی شفاعت قبول ہوتی ہے، اور فریادی ہے جس کی تصدیق ہوتی ہے۔ جو اسے اپنے آگے رکھے، وہ اسے جنت کی طرف لے جاتا ہے؛ جو اسے پیچھے چھوڑے، وہ اسے آگ کی طرف ہانکتا ہے۔ وہ رہنما ہے جو بہترین راستے کی طرف رہنمائی کرتا ہے۔'
translations.zh: 叙述者说：随后米格达德·本·阿斯瓦德起身说："啊，安拉的使者！停战的家园是什么？"他说："是传达与终结的家园。当考验像黑暗夜晚的碎片一样让你们困惑时，你们应当坚守《古兰经》，因为它是代祷被接受的代祷者，是被认证的控诉者。凡将其置于前者，它引导他进入乐园；凡将其置于后者，它驱使他进入火狱。它是指向最佳道路的向导。"

translations.bn: এটি এমন একটি কিতাব যাতে বিস্তারিত বিবরণ, ব্যাখ্যা এবং অর্জন রয়েছে। এটি সিদ্ধান্তকারী, রসিকতা নয়। এর প্রকাশ্য ও গুপ্ত উভয়ই আছে: প্রকাশ্য হল বিধান এবং গুপ্ত হল জ্ঞান; প্রকাশ্য সুন্দর এবং গুপ্ত গভীর। এর নক্ষত্রসমূহ রয়েছে, এবং তার নক্ষত্রের উপর আরো নক্ষত্র আছে। এর বিস্ময়সমূহ গণনা করা যায় না এবং বিরল বৈচিত্র্য নিঃশেষ হয় না।
translations.de: Er ist ein Buch, in dem sich Ausführlichkeit, Erklärung und Erwerb befinden. Er ist das Entscheidende, kein Scherz. Er hat ein Äußeres und ein Inneres: sein Äußeres ist ein Urteil und sein Inneres ist Wissen; sein Äußeres ist elegant und sein Inneres ist tief. Er hat Leuchten, und über seinen Leuchten gibt es Leuchten. Seine Wunder können nicht gezählt werden, noch erschöpfen sich seine Seltenheiten.
translations.en: It is a Book in which there is detail, explanation, and achievement. It is the decisive word, not jest. It has an outward and an inward: its outward is judgment and its inward is knowledge; its outward is elegant and its inward is profound. It has luminaries, and upon its luminaries are more luminaries. Its wonders cannot be counted nor do its rarities become exhausted.
translations.es: Es un Libro en el que hay detalle, explicación y logro. Es la decisión, no broma. Tiene un exterior y un interior: su exterior es mandato y su interior es conocimiento; su exterior es elegante y su interior es profundo. Tiene luminarias, y sobre sus luminarias hay más luminarias. Sus maravillas no pueden contarse ni sus particularidades se agotan.
translations.fa: آن کتابی است که در آن تفصیل، بیان و تحصیل است. آن سخن جداکننده است نه شوخی. برای آن ظاهر و باطنی است: ظاهرش حکم و باطنش علم است؛ ظاهرش زیباست و باطنش عمیق. آن را ستارگانی است و بر ستارگانش ستارگانی دیگر. شگفتی‌هایش شمرده نمی‌شود و غرایبش کهنه نمی‌گردد.
translations.fr: Il est un Livre en lequel se trouvent détail, explication et accomplissement. Il est le tranchant, non la plaisanterie. Il a un aspect extérieur et un aspect intérieur : son extérieur est un jugement et son intérieur est un savoir ; son extérieur est élégant et son intérieur est profond. Il possède des luminaires, et au-dessus de ses luminaires, il y a d'autres luminaires. Ses merveilles ne peuvent être dénombrées ni ses singularités épuisées.
translations.id: Ia adalah Kitab yang di dalamnya terdapat perincian, penjelasan, dan pencapaian. Ia adalah pemisah yang bukan gurauan. Ia mempunyai yang lahir dan yang batin: lahirnya adalah hukum dan batinnya adalah ilmu; lahirnya indah dan batinnya dalam. Ia mempunyai bintang-bintang, dan di atas bintang-bintangnya terdapat bintang-bintang lagi. Keajaibannya tidak dapat dihitung dan keanehannya tidak pernah habis.
translations.ru: Он — Книга, в которой есть подробность, объяснение и достижение. Он — решающее слово, не шутка. У него есть внешнее и внутреннее: его внешнее — это суждение, а его внутреннее — это знание; его внешнее изящно, а его внутреннее глубоко. У него есть звёзды, и над его звёздами звёзды. Его чудеса нельзя перечислить, и его редкости не истощаются.
translations.tr: O, içinde ayrıntı, açıklama ve kazanım bulunan bir Kitaptır. O, kesin sözdür, şaka değildir. Onun zahiri ve batını vardır: zahiri hükümdür, batını ise ilimdir; zahiri zarif, batını ise derindir. Onun yıldızları vardır ve yıldızlarının üstünde yıldızlar vardır. Harikalar sayılamaz, nadirlikler tükenmez.
translations.ur: یہ ایک کتاب ہے جس میں تفصیل، بیان اور تحصیل ہے۔ یہ فیصلہ کن ہے، مذاق نہیں۔ اس کا ظاہر اور باطن ہے: ظاہر حکم ہے اور باطن علم ہے؛ ظاہر جاذب ہے اور باطن گہرا ہے۔ اس کے نجوم ہیں، اور اس کے نجوم پر نجوم ہیں۔ اس کی عجائبات گنی نہیں جا سکتیں اور غرائب پرانی نہیں ہوتیں۔
translations.zh: 它是一部内含详述、阐释与成就的经典。它是裁判，而非玩笑。它有显义与隐义：其显义是律法，其隐义是知识；其显义优美，其隐义深奥。它有星辰，其星辰之上有更多星辰。其奇迹不可胜数，其奇特之处永不磨损。

translations.bn: এতে হেদায়েতের প্রদীপসমূহ, প্রজ্ঞার মিনার এবং তার গুণ-বৈশিষ্ট্য যে জানে তার জন্য মারেফাতের পথনির্দেশ রয়েছে। তাই স্পষ্টকারী তার দৃষ্টি উজ্জ্বল করুক এবং তার চিন্তন সেই সীমায় পৌঁছে দিক — ধ্বংস থেকে নাজাত পাবে এবং জটিলতা থেকে মুক্তি পাবে; কারণ চিন্তন হল অন্তর্দৃষ্টিসম্পন্নের হৃদয়ের জীবন, ঠিক যেমন আলোকিত ব্যক্তি অন্ধকারে আলো নিয়ে চলে। তাই তোমাদের উপর সৎ আন্তরিকতা এবং কম দ্বিধা আবশ্যক।
translations.de: In ihm befinden sich die Laternen der Führung, die Minarette der Weisheit und ein Führer zur Erkenntnis für denjenigen, der sein Attribut kennt. So soll der Suchende seinen Blick erhellen und seine Betrachtung zu jenem Attribut ausdehnen — er wird von Ruin gerettet und von der Verstrickung befreit, denn das Nachdenken ist das Leben des Herzens des Weitsichtigen, genauso wie der Erleuchtete in der Dunkelheit mit dem Licht geht. So obliegt euch Vortrefflichkeit in der Aufrichtigkeit und wenig Zögern.
translations.en: In it are the lanterns of guidance, the minarets of wisdom, and a guide to recognition for whoever knows its attribute. So let the seeker brighten his vision and extend his contemplation to its capacity — he will be rescued from ruin and freed from entanglement, for pondering is the life of the heart of the perceptive, just as the enlightened one walks in darkness with light. So upon you is excellence in sincerity and littleness of hesitation.
translations.es: En él hay lámparas de guía, minaretes de sabiduría, y una guía hacia el conocimiento espiritual para quien comprende su atributo. Que el buscador aclare su visión y lleve su contemplación hasta ese atributo — será rescatado de la ruina y liberado del enredo, pues la reflexión es la vida del corazón perspicaz, tal como el iluminado camina en las tinieblas con la luz. Así que sobre vosotros está la excelencia en la sinceridad y la escasez de vacilación.
translations.fa: در آن مشعل‌های هدایت، مناره‌های حکمت، و راهنما به معرفت برای کسی که صفت را بشناسد. پس باید جلاکننده‌ای بصیرتش را جلا دهد و نگاهش را به آن صفت برساند؛ از هلاکت نجات یابد و از گرفتاری رها شود. زیرا تفکر حیات قلب بصیر است؛ چنان‌که نورانی در تاریکی‌ها با نور راه می‌رود. پس بر شما باد به نیکویی رهایی و کمی انتظار.
translations.fr: En lui se trouvent les lanternes de la guidance, les minarets de la sagesse, et un guide vers la connaissance pour celui qui reconnaît son attribut. Que le chercheur clarifie sa vision et porte son regard jusqu'à cet attribut — il sera sauvé de la ruine et délivré du piège, car la réflexion est la vie du cœur clairvoyant, tout comme celui qui est éclairé marche dans les ténèbres avec la lumière. Alors sur vous est l'excellence de la sincérité et le peu de temporisation.
translations.id: Di dalamnya terdapat lentera-lentera petunjuk, menara-menara hikmah, dan petunjuk menuju ma'rifat bagi yang memahami sifatnya. Maka hendaklah penjelas memperjernihkan pandangannya dan memperluas renungannya hingga mencapai sifat itu — ia akan selamat dari kehancuran dan terbebas dari jerat, karena perenungan adalah kehidupan hati yang berwawasan, sebagaimana orang yang tercerahkan berjalan dalam kegelapan dengan cahaya. Maka wajib atas kalian ketulusan yang baik dan sedikit keraguan.
translations.ru: В нём светильники руководства, минареты мудрости и руководство к познанию для того, кто знает его атрибут. Пусть проясняющий прояснит своё зрение и доведёт свой взгляд до того атрибута — он спасётся от гибели и избавится от запутанности, ибо размышление — это жизнь сердца проницательного, подобно тому как просветлённый идёт в темноте со светом. Итак, вам надлежит превосходство в искренности и мало промедления.
translations.tr: İçinde hidayet kandilleri, hikmet minareleri ve sıfatı tanıyan için marifete rehberlik vardır. Öyleyse arayan basiretini aydınlatsın ve bakışını o sıfata ulaştırsın; helakten kurtulur ve sıkışıklıktan azade olur; zira tefekkür, basiretli kalbin hayatıdır; tıpkı aydınlananın karanlıklarda nur ile yürümesi gibi. Öyleyse size güzel bir kurtulma ve az bekleme gerekir.
translations.ur: اس میں ہدایت کے چراغ، حکمت کے مینار، اور صفت کو جاننے والے کے لیے معرفت کی رہنمائی ہے۔ پس وہ اپنی بصیرت کو روشن کرے اور صفت تک اپنی نگاہ پہنچائے، ہلاکت سے نجات پائے گا اور الجھن سے خلاصی ملے گی، کیونکہ تفکر بصیر کے دل کی زندگی ہے جیسے روشن ضمیر اندھیروں میں نور کے ساتھ چلتا ہے۔ پس تم پر حسن تخلص اور قلت تربص لازم ہے۔
translations.zh: 其中有引导的明灯、智慧的灯塔，以及对认识其品质者通向认知的指南。那么，清明者应当明亮其洞察力，并将其凝视延伸至那一境界——他将从毁灭中获救，从纠缠中解脱，因为深思是有洞察力者心灵的生命，正如被照亮者借助光明在黑暗中行走。所以你们应当以优良的诚心和减少犹豫来要求自己。

diacritics_changes: Added full tashkeel throughout the text
Standardized honorific صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ
Added diacritics to عَلَيْهِمُ السَّلَامُ in narrator chain

isnad_matn.isnad_ar: عَلِيُّ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنِ النَّوْفَلِيِّ عَنِ السَّكُونِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ آبَائِهِ عَلَيْهِمُ السَّلَامُ قَالَ
isnad_matn.matn_ar: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ إِنَّكُمْ فِي دَارِ هُدْنَةٍ وَأَنْتُمْ عَلَى ظَهْرِ سَفَرٍ وَالسَّيْرُ بِكُمْ سَرِيعٌ وَقَدْ رَأَيْتُمُ اللَّيْلَ وَالنَّهَارَ وَالشَّمْسَ وَالْقَمَرَ يُبْلِيَانِ كُلَّ جَدِيدٍ وَيُقَرِّبَانِ كُلَّ بَعِيدٍ وَيَأْتِيَانِ بِكُلِّ مَوْعُودٍ فَأَعِدُّوا الْجَهَازَ لِبُعْدِ الْمَجَازِ قَالَ فَقَامَ الْمِقْدَادُ بْنُ الْأَسْوَدِ فَقَالَ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ وَمَا دَارُ الْهُدْنَةِ قَالَ دَارُ بَلَاغٍ وَانْقِطَاعٍ فَإِذَا الْتَبَسَتْ عَلَيْكُمُ الْفِتَنُ كَقِطَعِ اللَّيْلِ الْمُظْلِمِ فَعَلَيْكُمْ بِالْقُرْآنِ فَإِنَّهُ شَافِعٌ مُشَفَّعٌ وَمَاحِلٌ مُصَدَّقٌ وَمَنْ جَعَلَهُ أَمَامَهُ قَادَهُ إِلَى الْجَنَّةِ وَمَنْ جَعَلَهُ خَلْفَهُ سَاقَهُ إِلَى النَّارِ وَهُوَ الدَّلِيلُ يَدُلُّ عَلَى خَيْرِ سَبِيلٍ وَهُوَ كِتَابٌ فِيهِ تَفْصِيلٌ وَبَيَانٌ وَتَحْصِيلٌ وَهُوَ الْفَصْلُ لَيْسَ بِالْهَزْلِ وَلَهُ ظَهْرٌ وَبَطْنٌ فَظَاهِرُهُ حُكْمٌ وَبَاطِنُهُ عِلْمٌ ظَاهِرُهُ أَنِيقٌ وَبَاطِنُهُ عَمِيقٌ لَهُ نُجُومٌ وَعَلَى نُجُومِهِ نُجُومٌ لَا تُحْصَى عَجَائِبُهُ وَلَا تُبْلَى غَرَائِبُهُ فِيهِ مَصَابِيحُ الْهُدَى وَمَنَارُ الْحِكْمَةِ وَدَلِيلٌ عَلَى الْمَعْرِفَةِ لِمَنْ عَرَفَ الصِّفَةَ فَلْيَجْلُ جَالٍ بَصَرَهُ وَلْيُبْلِغِ الصِّفَةَ نَظَرَهُ يَنْجُ مِنْ عَطَبٍ وَيَتَخَلَّصْ مِنْ نَشَبٍ فَإِنَّ التَّفَكُّرَ حَيَاةُ قَلْبِ الْبَصِيرِ كَمَا يَمْشِي الْمُسْتَنِيرُ فِي الظُّلُمَاتِ بِالنُّورِ فَعَلَيْكُمْ بِحُسْنِ التَّخَلُّصِ وَقِلَّةِ التَّرَبُّصِ

de.ظَهْرٌ وَبَطْنٌ: Äußeres und Inneres — die zwei Bedeutungsebenen des Qurans

en.التَّفَكُّرَ: Pondering/Reflection — the active engagement of the intellect with the Quran's meanings
en.دَارُ الْهُدْنَةِ: House of Truce — a metaphor for the temporary world, conceived as a ceasefire before the ultimate accounting

es.دَارُ الْهُدْنَةِ: Casa de tregua — metáfora del mundo como estación temporal
es.ظَهْرٌ وَبَطْنٌ: Exterior e interior — las dos capas de significado del Corán

fa.ظَهْرٌ وَبَطْنٌ: ظاهر و باطن — دو لایه معنایی قرآن

fr.ظَهْرٌ وَبَطْنٌ: Extérieur et intérieur — les deux couches de sens du Coran
